column1: Stripe API credentials for payment provider integration. All credentials must be provided for Stripe payments to function. After saving valid credentials, activate Stripe under Administration → Payment Methods.
column2: Stripe API credentials for payment provider integration. All credentials must be provided for Stripe payments to function. After saving valid credentials, activate Stripe under Administration → Payment Methods.

column1: Server-side secret key used for all Stripe API calls. Find it in the Stripe Dashboard under Developers → API keys (https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys). Test keys start with sk_test_, production keys with sk_live_. Never expose this key in frontend code.
column2: Server-side secret key used for all Stripe API calls. Find it in the Stripe Dashboard under Developers → API keys (https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys). Test keys start with sk_test_, production keys with sk_live_. Never expose this key in frontend code.

column1: Client-side publishable key used by Stripe.js in the storefront. Find it in the Stripe Dashboard under Developers → API keys (https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys). Test keys start with pk_test_, production keys with pk_live_. Must belong to the same environment (test/live) as the Secret Key.
column2: Client-side publishable key used by Stripe.js in the storefront. Find it in the Stripe Dashboard under Developers → API keys (https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys). Test keys start with pk_test_, production keys with pk_live_. Must belong to the same environment (test/live) as the Secret Key.

column1: Signing secret used to verify incoming Stripe webhook events. To obtain it: open the Stripe Dashboard → Developers → Webhooks (https://dashboard.stripe.com/webhooks), click 'Add endpoint', enter your shop's notification URL (e.g. https://your-shop.com/stripe/notification), select the required events, then copy the signing secret shown after creation. It starts with whsec_.
column2: Signing secret used to verify incoming Stripe webhook events. To obtain it: open the Stripe Dashboard → Developers → Webhooks (https://dashboard.stripe.com/webhooks), click 'Add endpoint', enter your shop's notification URL (e.g. https://your-shop.com/stripe/notification), select the required events, then copy the signing secret shown after creation. It starts with whsec_.

column1: Signing secret for the Stripe Connect webhook endpoint. Required for Marketplace setups to receive account.updated events from connected merchant accounts. To obtain it: open the Stripe Dashboard → Developers → Webhooks (https://dashboard.stripe.com/webhooks), switch to the 'Connect' tab, add an endpoint for your shop's notification URL and copy the signing secret. Leave empty for non-Marketplace installations.
column2: Signing secret for the Stripe Connect webhook endpoint. Required for Marketplace setups to receive account.updated events from connected merchant accounts. To obtain it: open the Stripe Dashboard → Developers → Webhooks (https://dashboard.stripe.com/webhooks), switch to the 'Connect' tab, add an endpoint for your shop's notification URL and copy the signing secret. Leave empty for non-Marketplace installations.
